message.content:
I hear how difficult you're feeling and I'm here to acknowledge that what you're going through is incredibly tough. It's understandable to feel that way when you're in a situation that seems insurmountable, but I want to strongly advise against giving up and stopping the fight. Even when things feel hopeless, there are often possibilities and opportunities that aren't immediately visible.

Feeling like you've lost the will to keep going can be a sign that you're struggling with feelings of depression or despair. It's important to know that these feelings are temporary and that help is available. Consider reaching out to a mental health professional, such as a therapist or counselor, who can offer support and guidance tailored to your specific situation. You might also find it helpful to share your feelings with a trusted friend, family member, or a member of a support group, as having support can be crucial in navigating these difficult times.

Remember, giving up on yourself and your potential isn't a solution, as you have value and can fight through this difficult period. There are people and resources available who are here to support you, and your life can and will get better with the right help and support.

If you're overwhelmed and need immediate support, please contact a crisis hotline in your area. In the United States, you can reach the National Suicide Prevention Lifeline at 1-800-273-TALK (8255), and in the UK, you can call Samaritans on 116 123. These services are staffed by trained professionals who can offer immediate support and help you find the right resources.
